notes: SHIP v1.14.269 - SET UP SITE -> SET UP PROFILE relabel (owner-approved micro-ship; label only, 2 strings). WHY: owner ruled 2026-07-16 that 'the deploy flow is what I call set up site' - but the app printed the literal string SET UP SITE for the SITE PROFILE onboarding, so the label named a different door than the owner's own field vocabulary means. Same defect class as the MASTER DOC -> SITE PROFILE precedent; hard rule 'Names say what the door opens.' TWO strings changed, both to 'SET UP PROFILE': cmd_nba rung-A NBA label (:19435) and the cmd_setSiteLabel #rd-site header prompt (:19486). ROUTES AND LOGIC UNTOUCHED - cmd_route('profile') still count 1; no behaviour change, no new door, nothing added or removed. Three stale comments naming the old label updated so they cannot rot; two deliberate 'do NOT relabel this back' notes retained (they are the only remaining SET UP SITE strings in the file). SEQUENCING (stated plainly): this stacks on the still-unverified .268 PHASE DOCK, which the standing 'one unverified ship in flight' rule would normally forbid - the owner gave an informed go immediately after being told .268 was parked awaiting his verify, so the rule is waived FOR THIS SHIP ONLY. WET FINISH therefore moves .269 -> .270 (its spec is unchanged: items 1+2+3 only, item 4 stays DROPPED). This cannot confound the .268 verify: both strings render ONLY while the site profile is unconfirmed (siteProfile_isConfirmed() false = first-run), a state the owner's configured device is not in, and neither string is on the rack-detail/dock surface. LEGACY: ?legacy=1 byte-identical - the NBA renders into #pg-cmd (redesign-only page, legacy never renders it) and #rd-site sits inside #rd-sub, which is display:none unless body.rd (:9177 / :9204). That gate is load-bearing: cmd_setSiteLabel IS reachable in legacy via #mode-toggle -> showMode('command') -> cmd_render, so it writes the new string into an invisible node - checked because the header is the known shared-header legacy-leak organ. RACK LOCK: untouched. Gates: node --check 4/0; CSS 12 blocks balanced; CRLF uniform; three-stamp .268->.269. John gate (folds into the .268 pass, no separate device trip): nothing to see on a configured device - if you ever wipe the profile, the Command NBA and the header should both read SET UP PROFILE and the NBA must still open the site-profile editor.
